message:
So my mom showed up at my place last weekend with a go-bag in her car—you know, just in case. And I *should* have rolled my eyes, except... I remembered the time our power went out for three days and I was ransacking drawers at 11 PM looking for a flashlight.

Turns out being "that person" who has a little emergency kit actually feels kind of good? No stress, no scrambling, just "oh, I've got this."

I'm not saying you need to become a full-blown prepper (though honestly, no judgment if you do). But having even a small bag with basics—water, meds, documents—sitting in a closet? That's the sweet spot between prepared and, you know, still having a normal life.

Do you keep a go-bag ready, or are you more of a "I'll figure it out when it happens" person?
